assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: The response does not mention checking for deprecated APIs or API version changes that could cause behavior differences in 1.30

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: The response does not mention admission webhooks as a potential cause of API latency or 503 errors

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: The response specifically mentions that v1.30 has higher memory requirements and suggests checking/increasing control plane resources, plus provides example YAML for adjusting resource requests/limits

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: The response includes commands to check kube-system pods status with 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep apiserver' and suggests restarting core components

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: The response includes multiple kubectl get --raw /metrics commands for checking API server flow control, request duration, and other metrics

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: The response mentions that v1.30 may have stricter network policies and includes commands to check NetworkPolicies, though it doesn't specifically mention service mesh components

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: The response includes commands to check events for errors and specifically mentions checking readiness probes with 'kubectl describe pods' commands

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: The response does not mention HPA (Horizontal Pod Autoscaler) or VPA (Vertical Pod Autoscaler) as potential sources of issues after the upgrade
